stakeholders.inference_basis: Brief: 'residential customers can view their monthly bills and pay them online by debit or credit card' - the portal exists for this actor.

stakeholders.role: Customer support agent handling disputes and lockouts
stakeholders.inference_basis: Brief: 'the customer support team takes over payment disputes, failed payments, and account-lockout tickets raised through the portal'.
Brief: 'a small platform team carries the on-call pager for portal availability' - they run the system after launch.
Brief: 'card payments are processed through an external payment gateway vendor' - an external dependency on the critical payment path.
Brief: 'the portal runs on a public cloud hosting provider under an existing enterprise agreement'.

stakeholders.role: National data protection authority (GDPR supervisory authority)
stakeholders.inference_basis: Inferred from domain: brief states bills contain 'personal data of residents in an EU member state', which activates GDPR and its supervisory authority (verify jurisdiction).

stakeholders.role: Card schemes via acquiring bank (PCI DSS compliance regime)
stakeholders.inference_basis: Inferred from domain: card payments imply contractual PCI DSS obligations enforced through the acquirer, even though 'Aurora does not want to store card numbers itself' (SAQ scope still applies - verify current PCI DSS version).
Brief: 'the energy market regulator audits billing accuracy annually' and launch is tied to a 'regulated billing-cycle change'.
Brief: 'the CFO is funding the initiative and expects it to cut call-center payment handling by half within a year of launch'.
Brief: 'paper billing continues for customers who do not enroll; the portal must not degrade service for offline customers, many of them elderly' - affected without using the system.
